summary: Excellent audience recording of the legendary 5/20/73 Santa Barbara show, highlighted by a smoking Truckin > The Other One > Eyes of the World sequence and Jerry’s unusually sweet vocals.
review: Fans rave about this 1973 Santa Barbara performance, especially the soaring Truckin‑Other One‑Eyes of the World stretch that many rank among the year’s best. The audience tape is praised as crystal‑clear—so clear that the vocal blend of Jerry, Phil and Bill actually sounds better than on most soundboard releases—making it a must‑listen for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews are strongly positive, emphasizing both an outstanding musical performance and superior audience recording quality; the few minor critiques do not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape praised for exceptional clarity and balance, especially on vocals; reviewers note that the mix sounds better than many soundboard releases and no drop‑outs or major flaws are reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows organically with a cosmic vibe; the middle jam (Truckin‑Other One‑Eyes) is highlighted as a peak, and the overall pacing is described as memorable and uplifting.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His singing is called ‘sweet’ and his vocal presence shines in this audience mix.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass blends well in the audience recording; the vocal‑bass balance is praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming sounds especially good on the soundboard, but overall drum feel is appreciated in the audience tape.
